title: hydrangea aspera subsp. strigosa
intro:
Hydrangea aspera subsp. strigosa, commonly known as Mountain Hydrangea, is a flowering plant species in the family Hydrangeaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hydrangea aspera subsp. strigosa include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its therapeutic actions of reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties.

preparations: Hydrangea aspera subsp. strigosa has many herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which can be used for various medicinal purposes. The tea made from the plant is used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and diarrhea, while also being used as a mild laxative. The tincture, on the other hand, is used to treat menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome (PMS), as well as being used as a general anti-inflammatory agent. side_effects: Hydrangea aspera subsp. strigosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high levels of glycosides and volatile oils.
